food_subgroup: Mixed rice bowl with fried meat

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap is approximately 450 g, comprising a bowl of steamed white rice topped with a breaded and fried chicken cutlet, assorted seasoned vegetables, and gochujang sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Bibimbap with meat — composite dish estimation based on USDA FoodData Central ingredient data and HPB Singapore mixed rice dish references
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from a weighted composite of: cooked white rice (USDA FDC #168878), breaded fried chicken (USDA FDC #171477), mixed stir-fried vegetables (USDA FDC #169967), gochujang sauce (USDA FDC #2003573), and sesame oil (USDA FDC #172337). Proportions estimated as: rice 45%, chicken cutlet 30%, vegetables 15%, sauce/oil 10%. Per-100g values derived from weighted average of these components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — mixed rice dishes and bibimbap analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap. GI estimated at 54 based on the closest analogous entry: Korean bibimbap with mixed vegetables and rice, which has a reported GI of approximately 53–55 in the University of Sydney GI Database and supporting peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021, Diabetes Care). The addition of a fried chicken cutlet and gochujang sauce does not materially alter the GI relative to the rice-dominant base. GI category is low (≤55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 92.25) / 100 ≈ 49.8, rounded to 49.9. Carbohydrate per serving = 20.5 g/100g × 450 g = 92.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 310 mg per 100 g; a full 450 g serving delivers approximately 1395 mg sodium
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a single serving of Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap may contribute approximately 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced gochujang sauce and soy-based seasonings can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High total carbohydrate load per serving (approximately 92 g) despite a low GI of 54, resulting in a high glycemic load per serving of approximately 50
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should note that while the glycemic index of Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap is low (GI 54), the large portion of white rice produces a high glycemic load per serving; reducing the rice portion by half and increasing the vegetable component can substantially lower postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from deep-fried breaded chicken cutlet and elevated sodium from gochujang and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap should be consumed in moderation due to its fried chicken component contributing saturated fat and its sauce contributing sodium; choosing a grilled or baked chicken variant and requesting less sauce are recommended modifications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap (닭가스 비빔밥, dakgaseu bibimbap) is a Korean rice bowl dish that combines the classic bibimbap format with a breaded and deep-fried chicken cutlet (dakgaseu, analogous to Japanese chicken katsu). The dish consists of a base of steamed short-grain white rice topped with an assortment of seasoned and blanched vegetables — typically spinach, bean sprouts, julienned carrots, and mushrooms — along with a crispy fried chicken cutlet, and is finished with gochujang (Korean fermented chilli paste) and sesame oil. The dish is mixed together before eating, which is the defining characteristic of bibimbap (literally 'mixed rice'). The chicken cutlet provides a contrasting crispy texture against the soft rice and tender vegetables, while the gochujang delivers a savoury, mildly spicy, and slightly sweet flavour profile. Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap is a popular modern Korean comfort food served widely in Korean casual dining restaurants and food courts across Singapore, including at establishments such as Mincheng Bibimbap. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy, protein-containing mixed rice bowl with approximately 165 kcal per 100 g and 9.2 g protein per 100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at 54 (low GI), though the glycemic load per full serving is high due to the large rice portion. Sodium content is elevated due to gochujang and seasoning, making portion control relevant for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken bibimbap?
Korean rice bowl with fried chicken calories
Is chicken bibimbap high in sodium?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of approved USDA FoodData Central ingredient entries. GI is estimated from the closest analogous bibimbap entry in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level data sources are high quality and the dish composition is well-defined.
notes: Sentinel value documentation: vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 because vitamin D is not reported in any of the USDA FoodData Central component entries used for this composite estimation (cooked white rice FDC #168878, breaded fried chicken FDC #171477, mixed stir-fried vegetables FDC #169967, gochujang sauce FDC #2003573, sesame oil FDC #172337); the value is genuinely absent from the source data and cannot be reliably estimated. All other per_100g fields are numeric. Nutrient values represent a per-100g weighted average of the estimated dish composition: steamed white rice 45%, breaded fried chicken cutlet 30%, mixed seasoned vegetables 15%, gochujang sauce and sesame oil 10%. GI of 54 is an analogous estimate based on Korean bibimbap with mixed vegetables from the University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). Glycemic load per serving = (54 × 92.25) / 100 ≈ 49.9, where carbohydrate per serving = 20.5 g/100g × 450 g serving = 92.25 g. Halal status is marked false as the restaurant (Mincheng Bibimbap) does not carry a confirmed MUIS halal certification based on available information; this should be verified. The input food_name '鸡排饭 Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap' has been normalised to canonical English 'Chicken Cutlet Bibimbap'; the Chinese characters 鸡排饭 are preserved in aliases and local_names.

Iteration 3: Rewrote the notes field to open with an explicit 'Sentinel value documentation:' heading for vitamin_d_mcg = -1, naming each specific USDA FDC component entry that does not report vitamin D, making the missing-data basis unambiguous and directly satisfying the G3 sentinel values documentation rule.
